ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a beef-and-mushroom melt submarine sandwich meal set with cookie. FOODON_00002473 (submarine sandwich) is the closest parent category. Cookie side item is not captured.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Sandwich) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for beef-mushroom melt sub meal set with cookie in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific sandwich meal set product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal set consisting of a 6-inch beef and mushroom melt submarine sandwich (approximately 250 g) plus one standard Subway cookie (approximately 45–50 g), totalling approximately 300–320 g per meal.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Steak & Cheese 6-inch sub and Subway Chocolate Chip Cookie — USDA Branded Food / Subway published nutrition data adapted for Beef Mushroom Melt Meal Set
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central — Branded Foods; Subway Restaurants Nutrition Information
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for 'Beef and Mushroom Melt Cookie Meal Set' as served in Singapore. Values are adapted from USDA Branded Food entries for Subway Steak & Cheese 6-inch sub (USDA FDC #: representative steak sub entries) and Subway Chocolate Chip Cookie, combined and normalised to per-100 g basis for a ~320 g meal set. Beef mushroom melt is nutritionally analogous to a steak-and-cheese sub with mushroom topping. Protein, fat, carbohydrate, and energy values are weighted composites. Sodium reflects the high-sodium profile typical of Subway sub sandwiches. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white bread sub roll and mixed sandwich meal — analogous estimate based on white bread GI and composite meal GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Subway Beef and Mushroom Melt Cookie Meal Set. GI estimated at 56 based on: (1) white wheat sub roll GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database, white bread reference), (2) protein and fat content of beef and cheese components lower the composite meal GI, (3) cookie component raises sugar contribution. Composite meal GI estimated at ~56 (medium) consistent with published mixed sandwich meal GI data from University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 100.8 g carbohydrate in 320 g serving) / 100 ≈ 56.4.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~580 mg per 100 g; ~1856 mg per meal set)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal set due to its very high sodium content (~1856 mg per serving), which represents approximately 80% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; choosing a lower-sodium bread option and omitting processed cheese can partially reduce sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~56) and high glycemic load per serving (~56.4) driven by refined wheat sub roll and cookie
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this meal set due to its high glycemic load (~56.4 per serving) from the refined wheat bread and cookie; selecting a wholegrain bread option and omitting the cookie can substantially reduce the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.4 g per 100 g), trans fat (~0.2 g per 100 g), and high sodium from processed beef and cheese
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this meal set due to its saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium content; the processed cheese and beef strips contribute to LDL-raising saturated fat intake, and the high sodium load increases cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Beef and Mushroom Melt Cookie Meal Set is a Western-style fast-food sandwich meal available at Subway outlets in Singapore, including Subway Funan and the Funan Mix & Match outlet. The meal set consists of a 6-inch submarine sandwich filled with seasoned beef strips, sautéed mushrooms, and melted processed cheese, served on a choice of bread (subject to availability), accompanied by a standard Subway cookie (typically chocolate chip or oatmeal raisin). The sub is assembled with fresh vegetables such as lettuce, tomato, onion, and capsicum, and may be toasted. The flavour profile is savoury and umami-rich from the beef and mushroom combination, with a creamy, slightly salty note from the melted cheese, and a sweet finish from the cookie. Nutritionally, this meal set is energy-dense (~248 kcal per 100 g; ~794 kcal per meal set), high in refined carbohydrates from the wheat bread and cookie, moderate in protein from the beef, and high in sodium (~580 mg per 100 g; ~1856 mg per serving). The glycemic index is estimated at 56 (medium), with a high glycemic load per serving (~56.4), making it a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The high sodium content is a significant concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The meal is halal-certified at Subway Singapore. Bread options are subject to availability. Priced at approximately SGD 16.00 on field survey data.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in any approved primary source for this specific Subway Singapore meal set product. Values are adapted from USDA Branded Food entries for analogous Subway steak-and-cheese sub and Subway cookie, normalised to per-100 g for a ~320 g composite meal. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database white bread and mixed sandwich meal data. Confidence is medium due to the composite and adapted nature of the nutrient derivation.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on USDA Branded Food data for Subway Steak & Cheese 6-inch sub and Subway Chocolate Chip Cookie, normalised per 100 g for a ~320 g meal set. No Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry was found for this product. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source entries used for this adaptation. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source entries used for this adaptation. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source entries used for this adaptation. 5. Trans fat (~0.2 g per 100 g) is present at low levels from processed beef and cheese components, consistent with USDA Branded Food data for similar Subway products. 6. Bread options are subject to availability per the original field survey listing; nutritional values assume standard white Italian sub roll. 7. The food_group has been corrected from the input value 'Vegetables' to 'Mixed', which accurately reflects the composite nature of this sandwich meal set. 8. GI is estimated at 56 (medium) based on analogous white bread sub roll and mixed sandwich meal GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database; this is not a direct measurement. 9. Halal status reflects Subway Singapore's halal certification; kosher status is not applicable. 10. The cookie component is assumed to be a standard Subway cookie (~45–50 g); actual cookie type may vary by outlet.
